zotero.abstractNote: How well do humans and machines perceive 3D shape and affordances from static images? We introduce the 'Hole-in-the-wall' task, which requires observers to judge whether a 3D object (a 'key') can pass through a hole in a wall. This task requires an understanding of 3D shape and the physical constraints of the environment. We find that humans are highly accurate at this task, whereas state-of-the-art deep neural networks struggle, even when trained on large datasets of 3D objects. Our results suggest that current computer vision models lack a robust representation of 3D shape and physical affordances.
